data.title: [worktree-integration]: fix-bug and debug-everything dispatch isolation:worktree without a harvest/integration path -> agent results orphaned and cleanup impossible
data.description:
## Background

Surfaced during investigation of bug afdb-8418 (agent worktrees persist after session ends). afdb-8418 is being resolved with two narrow fixes at the script level (harvest-worktree.sh self-cleanup + worktree-cleanup.sh agent reclamation). Those fixes close the user-visible symptom (accumulation) for the sprint path but do NOT address a deeper structural gap uncovered during analysis.

This epic captures the deeper gap for later investigation and fix.

## Problem Statement

/dso:fix-bug (lines 399 and 794 of plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md) and /dso:debug-everything (lines 73-79, 119-125, 810 of plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md) dispatch sub-agents with isolation="worktree" when worktree.isolation_enabled=true, but neither skill has:

1. A step that calls harvest-worktree.sh to merge the agent's branch into the session branch.
2. A step that reads the agent worktree's artifacts (review-status, test-gate-status) that harvest-worktree.sh requires.
3. A step that integrates the agent's fix before the orchestrator's gates (Step 7 Verify, Step 7.1 Scope-Drift, Gates 2a-2d, Step 7.5 Anti-Pattern Scan, Step 7.6 Test Index Check, Step 8 Commit) run on the diff.

Today, /dso:fix-bug "works" because of bug be6c-ed89 — the platform isolation parameter is inconsistently enforced, so fix sub-agents often write to the orchestrator's CWD anyway and the orchestrator's Step 7/8 pick them up accidentally. When isolation IS enforced, the fix is stranded in an agent worktree that nothing ever harvests or cleans up.

## Why Simple Reference to per-worktree-review-commit.md Doesn't Work

Initial proposal: have fix-bug and debug-everything reference plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md instead of duplicating cleanup instructions. Walked through scenarios — this does not compose cleanly.

### per-worktree-review-commit.md structure (7 steps)

| Step | Purpose | Classification |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Enter worktree context (WORKTREE_PATH, WORKTREE_ARTIFACTS) | Generic |
| 2 | Review INSIDE agent worktree via /dso:review | Sprint-specific |
| 3 | Record test status from worktree context | Generic |
| 4 | Commit INSIDE agent worktree via COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md | Sprint-specific |
| 5 | Harvest agent branch into session branch (harvest-worktree.sh) | Generic |
| 6 | Handle harvest result (conflict queue for multi-worktree batch) | Sprint-batch-specific |
| 7 | Worktree cleanup (git worktree remove + git branch -d) | Generic |

### Scenario A — Reference per-worktree-review-commit.md as-is in fix-bug Step 6/8

1. Fix sub-agent returns worktree_path, worktree_branch.
2. per-worktree Step 2 fires → /dso:review runs INSIDE agent worktree → produces review-status:passed.
3. per-worktree Step 4 fires → commits fix in agent branch via COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md.
4. per-worktree Step 5 → harvest merges to session branch, Fix 1 cleans up.
5. fix-bug resumes at Step 7 (Verify Fix): TEST_CMD passes.
6. fix-bug Step 7.1 (scope-drift reviewer): reads git diff — already committed, working-tree diff is empty. BREAKS.
7. fix-bug Gates 2a-2d: all read working-tree diff — empty. BREAK or misfire.
8. fix-bug Step 7.5 (anti-pattern scan): reads affected files from investigation, out-of-sequence.
9. fix-bug Step 8 (Commit): /dso:commit → nothing to commit; /dso:review re-runs. DOUBLED REVIEW.

**Verdict**: the two sequences are incompatible. per-worktree commits BEFORE its review+harvest; fix-bug commits AFTER its gates. Composing them breaks all five post-fix gates.

### Scenario B — Extract only Steps 1 + 7 into a new shared prompt (cleanup-only reference)

1. fix-bug dispatches fix sub-agent with isolation → fix lands in agent worktree.
2. fix-bug's Step 7 (Verify) runs TEST_CMD in orchestrator CWD — but the fix is in the agent branch, not the session branch. The test sees the un-fixed code.
3. A cleanup-only reference has nothing to clean up after, because there is no upstream harvest call.

**Verdict**: cleanup-only reference doesn't solve the cleanup problem because there is no integration step to clean up after. The agent worktree either sits empty (if isolation worked) or the orchestrator's CWD has the fix AND a stale empty agent worktree+branch still exists (what be6c-ed89 observed today).

### Scenario C — Keep sequences separate; fix the underlying structural gap

The correct fix is to give fix-bug and debug-everything an integration mechanism that:

- Either: dispatches fix sub-agents WITHOUT isolation (they write to orchestrator CWD, which is the current de facto behavior due to be6c-ed89), and the isolation config is honored only in sprint's batch flow where review+commit happen inside the worktree.
- Or: introduces a lightweight "integrate agent result" protocol for single-agent flows (fix-bug, debug-everything bug-fix mode) that moves the agent's files from the agent worktree to the orchestrator's session worktree BEFORE fix-bug's post-fix gates run, then cleans up the agent worktree. This avoids the sequence collision with per-worktree-review-commit.md's review-then-commit pattern.
- Or: allows fix-bug and debug-everything to fall through to shared-directory dispatch even when worktree.isolation_enabled=true, documenting the sub-agent contract that single-agent fix flows are not isolated.

## Scope (for this epic)

1. Audit every Agent dispatch in /dso:fix-bug and /dso:debug-everything SKILL.md with isolation:"worktree". Determine whether the sub-agent output needs to flow back into the session branch before the orchestrator's next step.
2. Coordinate with bug be6c-ed89 (Worktree Isolation: sub-agent writes to session branch instead of isolated worktree) — the two bugs are symptoms of the same underlying platform/protocol gap.
3. Design one of the three structural approaches above (or a fourth).
4. Update fix-bug and debug-everything SKILL.md to close the gap.
5. Remove reliance on be6c-ed89's accidental-behavior in the fix-bug/debug-everything happy path.

## Out of Scope (handled elsewhere)

- Sprint's per-worktree-review-commit.md flow — already correct.
- harvest-worktree.sh post-merge cleanup — shipped in afdb-8418.
- worktree-cleanup.sh agent reclamation — shipped in afdb-8418.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: /dso:fix-bug with isolation:worktree produces a clean session-branch commit containing the fix AND leaves no agent worktree or worktree-agent-* branch behind.
- SC2: /dso:debug-everything with isolation:worktree on bugs from Bug-Fix Mode produces clean commits AND leaves no agent worktrees or branches behind.
- SC3: fix-bug's post-fix gates (Step 7, 7.1, Gates 2a-2d, 7.5, 7.6) run on the same diff that ends up in the session commit — i.e., their working-tree diff reads reflect the actual fix.
- SC4: The fix coordinates with bug be6c-ed89 — either resolves it as a duplicate, or the two tickets have non-overlapping scope and are both resolved.

## Linkage

- Parent of root cause: bug afdb-8418 (worktree+branch accumulation).
- Related: bug be6c-ed89 (isolation:worktree writes to session branch).
- References: plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md:399, plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md:794, plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md:73-79, 119-125, 810, plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md, plugins/dso/scripts/harvest-worktree.sh.

## Next Action

Run /dso:brainstorm on this epic to scope approach, design success criteria, and decompose into stories. This ticket is a placeholder.
